chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper provides a link to the Ranger code under an open-source license (MIT) at https://github.com/DependableSystemsLab/Ranger.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation relies entirely on standard software simulations (TensorFlow, TensorFI tool for fault injection) without physical hardware or large-scale deployment.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: A clear and explicit section detailing the 'Fault Model' is provided in Section II-C, stating precise assumptions (e.g., asynchronous network, up to f Byzantine nodes).
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides a rigorous, data-driven analysis of the performance trade-offs (e.g., quantifying the exact cost of replication, checkpointing overhead) in terms of memory and performance overheads during the inference phase.

major_weakness: The evaluation relies entirely on standard software simulations without physical hardware or large-scale deployment, which may limit the generalizability of the results.
